commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the remaining action is normal maintainer review, CI, and deciding whether the direct OpenAI live proof gap matters before merge.; Cleared: The diff only changes typed stream options, OpenAI Responses payload shaping, and tests; it does not add dependencies, CI permissions, secret handling, downloaded code, or package execution paths. (sha=c4c36ac1012d698164f52b3ad1fd17fba73aff74)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a replay-item-id option to the OpenAI Responses transport/wrapper path and updates regression tests so store-disabled requests omit prior server item IDs while direct OpenAI store-enabled requests preserve them.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows store-disabled Responses requests can still replay prior server-owned item IDs, and the PR body supplies redacted real gateway logs matching that failure; I did not rerun a live provider in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the remaining action is normal maintainer review, CI, and deciding whether the direct OpenAI live proof gap matters before merge.

target.title: fix: avoid replaying Responses item ids when store is disabled
target.body:
## Summary

- Avoid replaying persisted OpenAI Responses item ids when the outgoing request disables `store`.
- Keep reasoning/text/tool-call replay metadata usable without sending stale server-owned item ids.
- Preserve replay item ids for direct OpenAI Responses requests whose provider wrapper sends final `store: true`.
- Update Responses transport and wrapper regression tests for both `store: false` and `store: true` behavior.

## Why

When OpenClaw sends OpenAI Responses requests with `store: false`, server-side item ids from a previous turn are not persisted by the provider. Replaying those ids in the next turn can make the provider reject the request with:

```text
Item with id '...' not found. Items are not persisted when store is set to false.
```

This was observed after a long Codex/OpenAI Responses agent turn where the follow-up turn replayed prior reasoning/text/function-call item ids.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Follow-up turns for a long-running OpenClaw Codex agent session using `mycodex/gpt-5.5` failed because the next OpenAI Responses request sent `store: false` while replaying prior server-owned Responses item ids. The provider rejected the request with `Items are not persisted when store is set to false`.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw `2026.5.19`, `agent-coding`, `mycodex/gpt-5.5`, OpenAI Responses-compatible endpoint, gateway on loopback. The local runtime patch was equivalent to this PR's store-disabled replay-id omission.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Replayed the same previously failing session after applying the equivalent local runtime patch:

```text
/usr/bin/node /home/hang/.local/lib/node_modules/openclaw/openclaw.mjs agent \
  --agent agent-coding \
  --session-id 69af5810-7e91-4886-a9b7-89d6d94f3329 \
  --message '验证补丁：请只回复 patched-ok，不要调用工具。' \
  --timeout 180 \
  --json
```

**Evidence after fix:** Redacted runtime log / live output from the real gateway. Before the patch, the same session repeatedly failed:

```text
2026-05-21T09:11:12Z [openai-transport] [responses] error provider=mycodex api=openai-responses model=gpt-5.5 status=404 message=404 Item with id 'rs_[redacted]' not found. Items are not persisted when `store` is set to false. Try again with `store` set to true, or remove this item from your input.
2026-05-21T09:12:26Z [openai-transport] [responses] error provider=mycodex api=openai-responses model=gpt-5.5 status=404 message=404 Item with id 'rs_[redacted]' not found. Items are not persisted when `store` is set to false. Try again with `store` set to true, or remove this item from your input.
```

After the patch, the same-session replay emitted the requested output and no further store-disabled item-id rejection appeared:

```text
2026-05-21T09:16:15Z patched-ok
```

Post-check command/output:

```text
journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway.service --since '2026-05-21 09:15:00 UTC' --no-pager \
  | rg 'Items are not persisted|store is set to false|patched-ok'

2026-05-21T09:16:15Z patched-ok
```

**Observed result after fix:** The real follow-up turn completed and produced `patched-ok`; the gateway logs since the patched replay contained no new `Items are not persisted when store is set to false` errors.

**What was not tested:** A live `api.openai.com` direct OpenAI `store: true` replay was not run from this machine; that compatibility path is covered by the new regression test that preserves item ids when the provider wrapper requests store-enabled replay, plus the wrapper test that threads `replayResponsesItemIds: true` for direct OpenAI.

## Testing

- `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner-extraparams.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo`
target.author: zhanghang02

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
